Light Spring.foundation_undertone: Warm peach to light warm beige
Light Spring.jewelry_guide: Delicate gold, rose gold, pearl, coral beads
Light Spring.description: You have warm, light coloring. Soft pastels and warm ivories look beautiful on you.
Light Spring.tips: Stick to light, warm, and clear colors. Avoid anything too dark, heavy, or icy.

makeup_extended.foundation: Warm ivory to light warm beige
makeup_extended.eyeliner: Brown, warm bronze — avoid harsh black

work.formula: Ivory base + peach accent + gold jewelry

formal.formula: Warm blush gown or champagne — avoid white

casual.formula: Warm denim + peach or ivory top

date_night.formula: Coral or warm rose — luminous, not dark

beach.formula: Soft pastels — peach, mint, pale yellow

wedding_guest.formula: Champagne, blush, or warm pastel — never white or black

athletic.formula: Warm pastels — peach, coral, soft yellow

color_harmonies.monochromatic: Layer ivory, peach, and warm honey — all in the same warm-light family
color_harmonies.complementary: Pair warm peach tones with muted dusty teal-green for interest
color_harmonies.analogous: Combine pale yellow, peach, and soft coral for a sun-kissed look

Light Spring.wardrobe_formula: Build on warm ivory and camel as your neutrals. Add depth with peach, coral, and warm honey. One bold coral or apricot piece per outfit keeps the look current.

True Spring.jewelry_guide: Yellow gold, hammered gold, amber, coral, citrine
True Spring.description: You have warm, golden coloring. Peach, coral, and warm greens are your best friends.
True Spring.tips: Choose warm, saturated colors. Coral, salmon, and warm turquoise are perfect. Skip cool pastels.

wedding_guest.formula: Bright coral, warm yellow, or peach-gold

athletic.formula: Bright warm tones — coral, orange, warm yellow

color_harmonies.monochromatic: Layer peach, apricot, and coral in different textures
color_harmonies.complementary: Pair warm coral/orange with teal-green for vibrant contrast
color_harmonies.analogous: Combine warm yellow, peach, and coral — three steps on the warm wheel

True Spring.wardrobe_formula: Camel and cognac as your neutrals. Coral and warm orange as your statement colors. Avoid grey and cool blue entirely — swap for warm teal or olive instead.

Warm Spring.foundation_undertone: Medium warm golden to warm tan
Warm Spring.jewelry_guide: Bold yellow gold, hammered brass, amber, turquoise in warm setting
Warm Spring.description: You have warm, clear coloring. Bold, bright colors with warm undertones suit you perfectly.
Warm Spring.tips: Go for rich, warm tones — burnt orange, olive, camel. Avoid cool blues and icy pinks.

makeup_extended.foundation: Warm medium beige to warm tan

color_harmonies.monochromatic: Layer caramel, terracotta, and burnt orange in tonal head-to-toe
color_harmonies.complementary: Pair burnt orange with deep warm teal for the richest contrast
color_harmonies.analogous: Combine golden yellow, terracotta, and warm rust for earthy depth

Warm Spring.wardrobe_formula: Terracotta, burnt orange, and warm olive are your power trio. Brown leather is your best neutral material. Keep one warm-camel piece in every outfit as your foundation.

Light Summer.foundation_undertone: Cool pink to cool light beige
Light Summer.jewelry_guide: Delicate silver, white gold, rose quartz, pearl, light amethyst
Light Summer.description: You have cool, delicate coloring. Soft lavenders, powder blues, and rose pinks flatter you.
Light Summer.tips: Choose soft, cool, muted tones. Dusty rose, lavender, and powder blue are ideal. Avoid harsh oranges.

makeup_extended.foundation: Cool light to cool neutral beige
makeup_extended.eyeliner: Soft grey, navy, lavender — avoid harsh black

color_harmonies.complementary: Pair soft rose with powder blue for a classic cool pairing
color_harmonies.analogous: Combine lavender, powder pink, and cool mauve for a romantic palette

Light Summer.wardrobe_formula: Build on soft grey and cool white as neutrals. Layer dusty rose, lavender, and powder blue. Avoid any warmth entirely — every piece should feel like a watercolor painting.

True Summer.foundation_undertone: Cool neutral beige to cool medium beige
True Summer.jewelry_guide: Sterling silver, white gold, grey pearl, amethyst, blue topaz
True Summer.description: You have cool, muted coloring. Dusty blues, soft grays, and cool mauves are ideal.
True Summer.tips: Go for soft, smoky tones with cool undertones. Avoid bright oranges and warm yellows.

color_harmonies.analogous: Combine lavender, dusty rose, and cool mauve for a sophisticated palette

True Summer.wardrobe_formula: Slate blue and cool grey as power neutrals. Dusty rose as your accent. Layer muted cool tones — never let a warm or bright color enter. Soft and sophisticated is your brand.

Soft Autumn.foundation_undertone: Warm neutral to medium warm beige
Soft Autumn.jewelry_guide: Matte gold, hammered bronze, amber, brown jasper, tortoiseshell
Soft Autumn.description: You have warm, muted coloring. Earthy olives, warm taupes, and terracotta suit you.
Soft Autumn.tips: Choose soft, warm earth tones. Camel, sage, terracotta work beautifully. Avoid electric and neon colors.

Soft Autumn.wardrobe_formula: Camel and warm taupe as your foundation neutrals. Terracotta and olive as accent colors. Think of a forest in early autumn — your wardrobe should feel like nature's palette.

True Autumn.jewelry_guide: Bold yellow gold, hammered copper, amber, tiger's eye, garnet
True Autumn.description: You have warm, rich coloring. Burnt orange, mustard, and deep teals are your palette.
True Autumn.tips: Go bold with warm, saturated earthy tones. Pumpkin, olive, and rust are stunning on you.

True Autumn.wardrobe_formula: Olive, dark brown, and cognac are your power neutrals. Burnt orange and forest green as your statement colors. Every outfit should look like autumn leaves — rich, warm, and deeply saturated.

Deep Autumn.jewelry_guide: Bold gold, raw brass, amber, garnet, deep coral, tiger's eye
Deep Autumn.description: You have warm, deep coloring. Rich, saturated warm tones like burgundy and forest green shine.
Deep Autumn.tips: Embrace deep, warm colors. Chocolate, burgundy, forest green, and bronze are your power colors.

Deep Autumn.wardrobe_formula: Deep brown, espresso, and dark olive are your neutrals. Oxblood and forest green as your statement colors. Light colors should be rare contrasts — warm ivory at most. Own your depth.

Deep Winter.jewelry_guide: Bold silver, white gold, black diamond, deep garnet, dark sapphire, onyx
Deep Winter.description: You have cool, deep coloring. Dramatic jewel tones — ruby, emerald, sapphire — are perfect.
Deep Winter.tips: Wear bold, deep, cool colors. Emerald, ruby, and royal blue create striking contrast with your features.

color_harmonies.monochromatic: Black + deep charcoal + near-black navy — total deep cool impact
color_harmonies.complementary: Black with pure white for maximum contrast, or emerald with ruby
color_harmonies.analogous: Deep navy, midnight plum, and emerald — cool jewel-tone harmony

Deep Winter.wardrobe_formula: Black is your ultimate neutral — own it completely. Add jewel tones as color statements: emerald, sapphire, ruby. Pure white for sharp contrast only. Never wear anything muted, warm, or pastels — you need drama.

True Winter.jewelry_guide: Bold silver, white gold, sapphire, clear diamond, cool emerald, platinum
True Winter.description: You have cool, icy coloring. Pure white, navy, and fuchsia create striking contrast.
True Winter.tips: Choose pure, vivid, cool colors. Navy, bright white, fuchsia, and emerald make you radiate.

True Winter.wardrobe_formula: Navy and black as powerful neutrals. Pure white for sharp contrast. Bold, vivid jewel tones — royal blue, fuchsia, true red — as your statement colors. Nothing soft, muted, or warm is allowed in your wardrobe.

Bright Winter.jewelry_guide: Bold silver, white gold, amethyst, clear crystal, bright sapphire, platinum
Bright Winter.description: You have cool, vivid coloring. High-contrast, electric shades make you glow.
Bright Winter.tips: Go for bright, icy, high-contrast colors. Electric blue, hot pink, and pure white are your signature.

Bright Winter.wardrobe_formula: Black and pure white as your graphic neutrals. Electric violet, hot pink, and electric blue as your statement colors. Bold color-blocking is your format. Muted is your enemy — always go bright or go home.
